title: Extract the duplicated overlap check
description: The half-open interval overlap rule - two intervals overlap when each one starts before the other ends - is implemented independently at least three times in bookit: in the reservation-conflict scan of handleCreateReservation (internal/api/reservations.go), in the weekly-window overlap validation of ValidateWindows (internal/booking/types.go), and in the reservation-versus-window comparison inside FreeSlots (internal/booking/availability.go). The duplication has already caused drift risk between admission and availability math. Unify the overlap rule into a single implementation in internal/booking and make all three sites use it, without changing observable behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A single overlap implementation exists in internal/booking with unit tests of its own.

The reservation-conflict check in internal/api/reservations.go, the window validation in internal/booking/types.go, and the reservation-versus-window comparison in internal/booking/availability.go all delegate to it; no independent restatement of the rule remains at those sites.
Observable behavior is unchanged: every existing test passes unmodified.
The package dependency direction is preserved: internal/booking imports nothing from internal/api or internal/store.
No HTTP API change, no schema change, no change under internal/store/migrations/.
go test ./... and go test -race ./... pass.
constraints: Behavior-preserving refactoring only; if you believe a site's boundary semantics are wrong, report it, do not change it here.
Follow AGENTS.md conventions and the existing naming style of internal/booking.
Keep the diff proportionate: unify the rule, do not redesign the availability engine.
non_goals: Fixing any suspected boundary bugs in the existing sites (behavior must be preserved bit-for-bit).
Generalizing beyond the three named sites.
Performance tuning of the overlap scan.

visible_validation.description: Every existing test passes unmodified, plus new unit tests for the extracted rule.
Suite passes as CI runs it.
Diff shows the three named sites delegating to one implementation.

expected_artifacts: A diff against the starting revision
Unit tests for the extracted overlap implementation

human_review_rubric.criterion: One rule, three call sites, zero behavior change
The abstraction fits all three representations without contortion
Unnecessary-change rate: nothing outside the overlap rule was touched
Design decision for bridging the differing representations was stated and justified

known_ambiguities: The three sites compare different representations (UTC instants at two sites, minutes-since-midnight at one); whether to unify via a generic helper, conversion to a common representation, or thin wrappers over one core rule is a design decision to justify.
Where in internal/booking the extracted rule should live (an existing file or a new one) is open.
expected_failure_modes: Silently flipping an inclusive/exclusive boundary at one site while unifying.
Inverting the dependency direction by importing api or store types into internal/booking.
